content: The Self-Refine framework's iterative self-feedback loop can be modified to anchor multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective self-refine paradigm that retains the original method's advantage of no additional training or supervision while fixing its tendency to converge on incremental, biased local improvements. This new paradigm would leverage conflicting self-judgments from the LLM's own inconsistent outputs to avoid over-refinement of mediocre initial solutions and force more diverse, global improvements to generated outputs.
